STATE OF THE WORLD FROM NPR Rescue turns to recovery in Venezuela
Rescue turns to recovery in Venezuela

July 2, 2026 • Thousands remain missing more than a week after the twin earthquakes struck Venezuela and hope for finding survivors is fading into and effort to recover remains. With little help from the government many families are taking it upon themselves to dig through the rubble to find their loved ones. We witness some these efforts on Venezuela’s northern coast.

LISTEN· 5:05 PLAYLIST Download Embed SelÃ§uk Acar/Anadolu/Getty Images LIVING BETTER Going outside in this heat? Follow these guidelines to stay safe July 2, 2026 • Man, it's a hot one! Don't go out in this summer's heat wave before you arm yourself with these tips and a really big water bottle.

Spencer Platt/Getty Images IMMIGRATION The U.S. healthcare system is in crisis. A Supreme Court ruling could make things worse July 2, 2026 • Mass deportations would be felt across hospitals and emergency rooms, which already face persistent staffing shortfalls. The long-term healthcare sector will suffer the greatest disruptions, experts say. Win McNamee/Getty Images EDUCATION Federal civil rights data holds schools accountable. Under Trump, it's 6 months late July 2, 2026 • The Education Department has long collected civil rights data about things like bullying, harassment and disability services in schools, but it hasn't made the latest information public.

LISTEN· 3:58 PLAYLIST Transcript Download Embed Transcript Baz Ratner/AP RELIGION Vatican declares Society of St. Pius X in schism, excommunicates bishops July 2, 2026 • The Vatican responded Thursday to a traditionalist society that consecrated bishops without the pope's consent, declaring the Society of St. Pius X in schism and excommunicating its bishops and priests. Danylo Antoniuk/AP UKRAINE INVASION — EXPLAINED Russia hits Ukraine's capital with a massive drone and missile attack, killing at least 18 July 2, 2026 • Russia launched a large-scale attack on Ukraine's capital overnight into Thursday, with ballistic and cruise missiles and drones, killing several people.

LISTEN· 3:33 PLAYLIST Transcript Download Embed Transcript iStock/Getty Images THROUGHLINE The story of July 4th is messier than you remember July 2, 2026 • Devastating compromises. Midnight rides. A nailbiter vote. Statue toppling riots… and the very real possibility of death. This July 4th, we're taking you inside the making of the Declaration of Independence and how, against all odds, a single document introduced the world to a new kind of nation. LISTEN· 34:19 PLAYLIST Download Embed Amirhosein Khorgooi/ISNA via AP MIDDLE EAST U.S. and Iran hold separate meetings in Qatar and agree to continue discussions July 2, 2026 • U.S.

and Iranian negotiators met separately on Wednesday with Qatari and Pakistani mediators, with "positive progress made," and they agreed to continue discussions, host Qatar said. Danylo Antoniuk/AP WORLD A major Russian attack kills 17 in Kyiv as Ukraine keeps striking Moscow's oil sector July 2, 2026 • Loud explosions shook Kyiv for hours during the night, with many people sheltering at subway stations. Emergency crews were digging through the rubble of collapsed buildings in search of victims.

Ariana Cubillos/AP THE AMERICAS Untold casualties and humanitarian needs: What to know a week from Venezuela's quakes July 1, 2026 • Here's a look at some of the major developments since major back-to-back earthquakes rocked Venezuela on June 24, devastating parts of a country already reeling from crisis after crisis. Jacob Lujan/Houston Chronicle via Getty Images SOCCER EDITION Americans are showing up for the World Cup in record-breaking numbers July 1, 2026 • While the U.S. isn't a bona fide soccer nation yet, the past three weeks have perhaps shown what it would feel like if it were.

NPR+ EXCLUSIVE GET NPR+ Russell Gossett/NPR EMBEDDED We Keep Us Safe: Pocket’s World July 2, 2026 • EPISODE 4: Who was Antonio Mays Jr.? What was he up to at CHOP? LISTEN· 38:53 PLAYLIST Download Embed Andrew Harnik/Getty Images UP FIRST NEWSLETTER Trump denies conflict of interest over crypto. LISTEN· 13:23 PLAYLIST Transcript Download Embed Transcript Kevin Dietsch/Getty Images POLITICS Former ethics lawyer says Trump's crypto poses 'clear conflict of interest' July 2, 2026 • Former White House ethics lawyer Richard Painter says President Trump "stands alone" in having substantial financial conflicts of interest and that, "for every other executive branch official, it would be a violation."
